title: docker compose
content:
services:
  open-webui:
    image: ghcr.io/open-webui/open-webui:main
    container_name: open-webui
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "3000:8080"
    volumes:
      - open-webui:/app/backend/data
      # Change the left side to your ACTUAL Mac path
      - /Users/yourname/Documents/AI_Knowledge:/data/docs:ro
    environment:
      # General Settings
      - WEBUI_NAME=Lumi Control Center
      - WEBUI_AUTH=True
      - WEBUI_SECRET_KEY=generate_a_random_string_here  # Critical for session persistence
      
      # Connection to your native llama-server (Port 11436)
      - OPENAI_API_BASE_URL=http://host.docker.internal:11436/v1
      - OPENAI_API_KEY=none
      
      # RAG & Document Settings
      - DOCUMENTS_DIR=/data/docs
      - ENABLE_RAG=True
      - RAG_TOP_K=15
      
      # Privacy / Security
      - ENABLE_SIGNUP=False
    extra_hosts:
      - "host.docker.internal:host-gateway"
    # Optional: Limits to prevent Docker from getting too greedy with your 256GB RAM
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 8G

volumes:
  open-webui:

"-- Bundled by luabundle {"version":"1.7.0"}
local __bundle_require, __bundle_loaded, __bundle_register, __bundle_modules = (function(superRequire)
	local loadingPlaceholder = {[{}] = true}

	local register
	local modules = {}

	local require
	local loaded = {}

	register = function(name, body)
		if not modules[name] then
			modules[name] = body
		end
	end

	require = function(name)
		local loadedModule = loaded[name]

		if loadedModule then
			if loadedModule == loadingPlaceholder then
				return nil
			end
		else
			if not modules[name] then
				if not superRequire then
					local identifier = type(name) == 'string' and '\"' .. name .. '\"' or tostring(name)
					error('Tried to require ' .. identifier .. ', but no such module has been registered')
				else
					return superRequire(name)
				end
			end

			loaded[name] = loadingPlaceholder
			loadedModule = modules[name](require, loaded, register, modules)
			loaded[name] = loadedModule
		end

		return loadedModule
	end

	return require, loaded, register, modules
end)(nil)
__bundle_register("__root", function(require, _LOADED, __bundle_register, __bundle_modules)
--[[ Lua code. See documentation: https://api.tabletopsimulator.com

IMPORTANT! PLEASE READ THIS!

NOTE1: All TABLE CREATOR-related variables/functions use the naming convention "<variable>_TC" or "TC.<function>"
- ie. don't worry about variable/function naming conflicts so long as you do *not* use a suffix of "_TC" or prefix of "TC."

NOTE2: The library of utilities (useful functions for coding in TTS) use the prefix "Util." (ex. Util.get -> get object from GUID)
- see the fully documented list of available functions in the [UTILITIES] section
 
For built-in TABLE CREATOR-related functions, you can assign variables in the [TABLE CREATOR VARIABLES] section; here's what they do:

    AUTO-DRAW CARDS = Variables for automatically replenishing drawn cards from a row/column (see mod for live example)

    gSetupBoard_TC = GUID of custom board used for buttons (required: Objects -> Components -> Boards -> Custom Board)
    gSetupButton_TC = GUID of object used for setup button (example: Objects -> Components -> Go -> Black Piece)
    gRoundButton_TC = GUID of object used for round button (example: Objects -> Components -> Go -> Black Piece)
    gStartMarker_TC = GUID of object used as the start player marker (example: Objects -> Components -> Chess -> Wood -> King Light)
    gTurnMarker_TC = GUID of object used as the turn marker (example: Objects -> Components -> Chess -> Wood -> Pawn Light)

    tTurnOrder_TC = Indexed table of player colors (ex. "Red") used to change the turn order for the next round
        NOTE: Any seated/hotseat player colors *not* in this table will be appended to the end in their existing turn order

        tTurnOrder_TC Example #1:
        - Set new start player for the next round based on seated positions (ie. clockwise from start player)
        ---8<---8<---8<---
        1. Current round player turn order = [ Red, Blue, Green, White, Yellow ]
        2. tTurnOrder_TC = {"Green"}
        3. Next round player turn order = [ Green, White, Yellow, Red, Blue ]
        ---8<---8<---8<---    

        tTurnOrder_TC Example #2:
        - Set a player turn order *not* based on seated positions (ie. not *just* clockwise from start player)
        ---8<---8<---8<---
        1. Current round player turn order = [ Red, Blue, Green, White, Yellow ]
        2. tTurnOrder_TC = {"Red", "Green", "Yellow"}
        3. Next round player turn order = [ Red, Green, Yellow, Blue, White ]
        ---8<---8<---8<---    

    vSetupBoardURL_TC = URL of your custom image for the setup board (image size of 1024 x 1024 or greater recommended)
     
This global script is divided into the following sections:

    SAVE: Variable assignments entered in this section are saved to the JSON (see this section on how to save data between saving & loading the mod)
    LOAD: Variable assignments entered in this section are loaded from the JSON; also, use this section to assign any other global variables 
    STARTUP: After your global variables are assigned in the [LOAD] section, your code entered into the following sections is executed:
        1. STARTUP: Every time the mod is loaded your code entered in this section is executed 
        2. NO SETUP: If setup has *not* been completed your code entered in this section is executed
        3. SETUP: If setup has been completed your code entered in this section is executed
    SETUP: When the SETUP button is clicked your code entered in this section is executed
    ROUND: When the END ROUND button is clicked your code entered in this section is executed
    FUNCTIONS: Enter your functions that support your code in this section
    CREATE BUTTONS: Enter your button properties (position, size, color, function, etc.) in this section
    BUTTON FUNCTIONS: Enter the functions your buttons trigger, when clicked, in this section
    CREATE INPUTS: Enter your input properties (position, size, color, function, etc.) in this section
    INPUT FUNCTIONS: Enter the functions your inputs trigger, when text is entered, in this section
    TABLE CREATOR VARIABLES: Assign variables (GUIDs, positions, URLs, etc.) for TABLE CREATOR-related functions in this section
    EVENTS: For specific TTS events (ex. when an object leaves a container), your code entered into this section is executed
    UTILTIES: Functions in the utilities module (prefix "Util.") are documented (ex. Util.get -> get object from GUID) in this section

For mod-specific inquiries (ie. additions, requests, bugs, etc.), please feel free to comment on my steam profile here:
https://steamcommunity.com/profiles/76561198029197020/ (must be logged into steam)

For all other scripting-related inquiries, please join the Official Tabletop Simulator discord scripting channel found here:
https://discord.com/channels/342471570955960324/368208369812504587

]]--

local Util = require("lib.Util") -- Utilities module (table of functions)
local TC = require("lib.Table") -- Table creator (TC) module (table of functions)

-- Set all players as spectators
for _, oPlayer in pairs(Player.getPlayers()) do oPlayer.changeColor("Grey") end
broadcastToAll("IMPORTANT! *AFTER* MOD IS LOADED:\nSelect a player color to begin", "Grey")

--[[ SAVE ]]--

function onSave() -- Triggered when mod is saved

    --[[
    
    HOW TO SAVE DATA WHEN SAVING/LOADING THE MOD
    
    1. Enter data (variables, tables, etc.) in the [SET DATA] section below (see "bHappy = true" example)
       NOTE1: This is not necessary if the data has already been loaded into memory (ie. through code execution) before saving
       NOTE2: Object references (ex. oToken) can *not* be saved; save the object's GUID instead (ex. gToken)
              - Use the GUID to "get" the object reference in the onLoad section -> ex. oToken = Util.get(gToken)
    2. Enter variables as <JSON variable> = <CODE variable> into the [SAVE DATA] table below (see "Happy = bHappy" example)
    3. Enter variables as <CODE variable> = tData.<JSON variable> into the [LOAD DATA] section below (see "bHappy = tData.Happy" example)
       NOTE: Just in case the JSON's data isn't detected on load, enter data into the [DEFAULT DATA] section below (see "bHappy = true" example)
    4. Send script to TTS -> VSCode = [Ctrl]+[Alt]+[S]
       NOTE: Skip steps 5 & 6 if you didn't enter any data into the SET DATA section
    5. Save & reload the mod then remove data from the SET DATA section below (see "bHappy = true" example)
    6. Send script to TTS -> VSCode = [Ctrl]+[Alt]+[S]
    7. Voila! Data will now be saved to the JSON when saving the mod & loaded back into memory when loading the mod
    
    ]]--

    --[[ SET DATA ]]--
    -- bHappy = true

    local tSaveData = { -- Data to be saved to JSON file

        -- [[ SAVE DATA ]]--
        -- <JSON variable> (ex. Happy) = <CODE variable> (ex. bHappy)        
        -- Happy = bHappy

    }
    return JSON.encode(TC.onSave(tSaveData)) -- Encode data into JSON file
 end

--[[ LOAD ]]--

function onLoad(tEncodedData) -- Triggered when mod finishes loading
    if Util.is(tEncodedData) then
        local tData = JSON.decode(tEncodedData) -- Decode data from JSON file

        --[[ LOAD DATA ]]--
        -- <CODE variable> (ex. bHappy) = tData.<JSON variable> (ex. tData.Happy)        
        -- bHappy = tData.Happy

        TC.onLoad(tData) -- DO NOT DELETE! Load table-related data as well
    else

        --[[ DEFAULT DATA ]]--
        -- bHappy = true

        TC.defaults() -- DO NOT DELETE! Set table-related defaults as well
    end

    --[[ VARIABLES ]]--

    setVariables_TC() -- DO NOT DELETE! Table-related variables; see [TABLE CREATOR VARIABLES] below

    -- GUID: use "g" prefix to denote a GUID
    -- ex. gToken = "123456"

    -- OBJECT: use "o" prefix to denote an object
    -- ex. oToken = Util.get(gToken)

    -- TABLE: use "t" prefix to denote a table
    -- ex. tFruit = {"apple","orange","banana"}

    -- VARIABLE: use "v" prefix to denote a string (or combination of types)
    -- ex. vFruit = "apple"

    -- INTEGER: use "n" prefix to denote a number
    -- ex. nFruit = 3

	-- BOOLEAN: use "b" prefix to denote boolean
    -- ex. bFruit = true

    startLuaCoroutine(Global, "doStartup_TC") -- Triggers startup code (coroutine)
end

--[[ STARTUP ]]--

function doStartup_TC() -- Triggered after variables initialized (coroutine)
    TC.table("Startup") -- DO NOT DELETE! Table-related startup tasks
    if not tGameData_TC.setup then

        --[[

            "NO SETUP" CODE (COROUTINE):
            Enter code here to be executed every time the mod is loaded
            *AND* if setup has *NOT* been completed (ie. setup button *NOT* clicked)
            
            NOTE1: Saving the mod after setup is complete will encode that into the JSON file
            *AND* prevent this code from executing when the mod is loaded

            NOTE2: See [SETUP] section (below) to enter code to be executed
            when setup is triggered (ie. setup button is clicked)

            NOTE3: See [CREATE BUTTONS] section (below) to configure buttons
            for the setup board or table
            
            NOTE4: See [BUTTON FUNCTIONS] section (below) to enter functions
            to be performed when buttons are clicked

        ]]--

        TC.table("No-Setup") -- DO NOT DELETE! Table-related tasks if setup not yet triggered
    else

        --[[

            "SETUP DONE" CODE (COROUTINE):
            Enter code here to be executed every time the mod is loaded
            *AND* if setup *HAS* been completed (ie. setup button clicked)
            
            NOTE1: Saving the mod after setup is complete will encode that into the JSON file
            *AND* ensure the code in this section is executed when the mod is loaded

            NOTE2: See [ROUND] section (below) to enter code to be executed
            when the next round is triggered (ie. end round button clicked)

        ]]--

        TC.table("Setup-Done") -- DO NOT DELETE! Table-related tasks if setup has been triggered
    end

    --[[

        STARTUP CODE (COROUTINE):
        Enter code here to be executed every time the mod is loaded
        (ie. regardless of whether setup has been completed)

        NOTE1: See [EVENTS] section (below) to enter code to be executed
        when TTS-related events are triggered

    ]]--

    return 1
end

--[[ SETUP ]]--

function doSetup_TC() -- Executed when setup button clicked (coroutine)
    if #Util.seated() > 0 then
        TC.table("Pre-Setup") -- DO NOT DELETE! Table-related setup tasks *before* setup code routine

        --[[

            SETUP CODE (COROUTINE):
            Enter code here to be executed when the setup button is clicked

        ]]--

        TC.table("Post-Setup") -- DO NOT DELETE! Table-related setup tasks *after* setup code routine
    else
        Util.broadcast("Please select a player color!", "All", "Grey")
    end
    return 1
end

--[[ ROUND ]]--

function doRound_TC() -- Executed when end round button clicked (coroutine)
    if tGameData_TC.setup then
        TC.table("Pre-Round") -- DO NOT DELETE! Table-related round tasks *before* round code routine

        --[[

            ROUND CODE (COROUTINE):
            Enter code here to be executed when the end round button is clicked

        ]]--

        TC.table("Post-Round") -- DO NOT DELETE! Table-related round tasks *after* round code routine
    else
        Util.broadcast("Please perform setup first!", "All", "Grey")
    end
    return 1
end

--[[ FUNCTIONS ]]--

-- Enter your functions that support your code in this section

--[[ CREATE BUTTONS ]]--

-- Enter your button properties (position, size, color, function, etc.) in this section

function createButtons_TC(vType) -- Create buttons by type

	local tButton = { -- Default button parameters
        function_owner = Global, -- or "self" for objects
        click_function = "none", -- name of function triggered by click
        label = "", -- displayed name of button
        tooltip = "", -- text displayed during mouse-over
        width = 2750, -- width of button (relative to object)
        height = 1000, -- height of button (relative to object)
        color = "Green", -- color of button; either rgb (table) or player color (string)
        font_size = 400, -- size of label (relative to object)
        font_color = "Black", -- color of label; either rgb (table) or player color (string)
        --hover_color = "Grey", -- background color of button during mouse-over
        --press_color = "Black", -- background color of button when clicked
        position = {0, 0, 0}, -- position of button (relative to object center)
        rotation = {0, 0, 0}, -- rotation of button (relative to object's rotation)
        scale = {1, 1, 1}, -- scale of button (relative to object's scale)
    }

    -- Setup board buttons

    if vType == "SetupBoard" then
        -- NOTE: tButton.position -> set y = 0 to hide buttons but still clickable for customized setup boards (ex. {3, 0, 1.5})
        if Util.get(gSetupBoard_TC) then
            local oSetupBoard = Util.get(gSetupBoard_TC)

			-- DO NOT DELETE! This button required to execute setup
            tButton.click_function = "click_doSetup_TC" -- OK button (index 0)
			tButton.label = "OK"
			tButton.position = {0, 0.6, 5}
			tButton.tooltip = "Click after all players are seated"
			oSetupBoard.createButton(tButton)

            -- MODIFY/DELETE! Just here as examples ...
			tButton.click_function = "click_selectOption1_TC" -- Option #1 button (index 1)
			tButton.label = "[ ] Option #1"
			tButton.position = {-3, 0.6, 1.5}
			tButton.color = "Yellow"
			tButton.tooltip = "Example option button #1"
			oSetupBoard.createButton(tButton)

			tButton.click_function = "click_selectOption2_TC" -- Option #2 button 2 (index 2)
			tButton.label = "[ ] Option #2"
			tButton.position = {3, 0.6, 1.5}
			tButton.tooltip = "Example option button #2"
			oSetupBoard.createButton(tButton)
		end
    end
end

--[[ BUTTON FUNCTIONS ]]--

-- Enter the functions your buttons trigger, when clicked, in this section

-- Sets a global variable (boolean) to true/false if option enabled/disabled (see bBoardOption1_TC)
function click_selectOption1_TC(oButton, vPlayerColor, bAltClick) -- Executed when option button is clicked
    if not bAltClick then
        local oSetupBoard =  Util.get(gSetupBoard_TC)
		if bBoardOption1_TC then -- Disable option
            oSetupBoard.editButton({ index = 1, label = "[ ] Option #1" })
            Util.broadcast("Option #1: Disabled", "All", "Yellow")
			bBoardOption1_TC = false
		else -- Enable option
            oSetupBoard.editButton({ index = 1, label = "[x] Option #1" })
            Util.broadcast("Option #1: Enabled", "All", "Yellow")
			bBoardOption1_TC = true
		end
    end
end

-- Sets a global variable (boolean) to true/false if option enabled/disabled (see bBoardOption2_TC)
function click_selectOption2_TC(oButton, vPlayerColor, bAltClick) -- Executed when option button is clicked
    if not bAltClick then
        local oSetupBoard =  Util.get(gSetupBoard_TC)
		if bBoardOption2_TC then -- Disable option
            oSetupBoard.editButton({ index = 2, label = "[ ] Option #2" })
            Util.broadcast("Option #2: Disabled", "All", "Yellow")
			bBoardOption2_TC = false
		else -- Enable option
            oSetupBoard.editButton({ index = 2, label = "[x] Option #2" })
            Util.broadcast("Option #2: Enabled", "All", "Yellow")
			bBoardOption2_TC = true
		end
    end
end

--[[ CREATE INPUTS ]]--

-- Enter your input properties (position, size, color, function, etc.) in this section

function createInputs_TC(vType) -- Create inputs by type

    local tInput = { -- Default input parameters
        function_owner = Global, -- or "self" for objects
        label = "", -- greyed out text if no value input
        input_function = "none", -- name of function triggered by every letter typed
        value = "", -- text entered into input
        tooltip = "", -- text displayed during mouse-over
        width = 400, -- width of input (relative to object)
        height = 400, -- height of input (relative to object)
        color = "White", -- input's background color; either rgb (table) or player color (string)
        font_size = 100, -- size of label (relative to object)
        font_color = {0, 0, 0}, -- color of input text; either rgb (table) or player color (string)
        position = {0, 0, 0}, -- position of input (relative to object center)
        rotation = {0, 0, 0}, -- rotation of input (relative to object's rotation)
        scale = {1, 1, 1}, -- scale of input (relative to object's scale)
        alignment = 1, -- text alignment (1=Automatic, 2=Left, 3=Center, 4=Right, 5=Justified)
        validation = 1, -- permitted character types (1=All, 2=Integer, 3=Float, 4=Alphanumeric, 5=Username, 6=Name)
        tab = 1, -- if TAB is pressed (1=Nothing, 2=Next Input, 3=Indent)
    }

end

--[[ INPUT FUNCTIONS ]]--

-- Enter the functions your inputs trigger, when text is entered, in this section

--[[ TABLE CREATOR VARIABLES ]]--

function setVariables_TC() -- Set TABLE CREATOR-related variables here

    -- AUTO-DRAW CARDS
    -- Place decks on Deck GUID Fixer, click FIX DECK, then save/reload mod to ensure each deck's cards have a unique GUID

    nCardPiles_TC = 2 -- # of auto-drawn card piles

    -- Card Pile #1
    bDrawEndTurn1_TC = false -- auto-draw cards end of player turn only?
    gDiscardZone1_TC = "4aedaf" -- GUID of scripted zone of discard pile
    gDrawZone1_TC = "59f9f2" -- GUID of scripted zone of draw pile
    gCardZone1_TC = "98040b" -- GUID of scripted zone of auto-drawn cards
    tDrawPos1_TC = {-3.75, 1.23, -9.75} -- Position of draw pile
    tCardPos1_TC = { -- Position of each auto-drawn card (sequential order)
        {-1.25, 1.00, -9.75},
        {1.25, 1.00, -9.75},
        {3.75, 1.00, -9.75},
        {6.25, 1.00, -9.75},
    }

    -- Card Pile #2
    bDrawEndTurn2_TC = false -- auto-draw cards end of player turn only?
    gDiscardZone2_TC = "925645" -- GUID of scripted zone of discard pile
    gDrawZone2_TC = "c80609" -- GUID of scripted zone of draw pile
    gCardZone2_TC = "1cead9" -- GUID of scripted zone for auto-drawn cards
    tDrawPos2_TC = {-9.75, 1.02, 2.25} -- Position of draw pile
    tCardPos2_TC = { -- Position of each auto-drawn card (sequential order)
        {-9.75, 1.00, -0.25},
        {-9.75, 1.00, -2.75},
        {-9.75, 1.00, -5.25},
    }

    -- GUID

    gSetupBoard_TC = "be5bbe" -- GUID of custom board used for buttons (required: Objects -> Components -> Boards -> Custom Board)
    gSetupButton_TC = "dbb918" -- GUID of object used for setup button (example: Objects -> Components -> Go -> Black Piece)
    gRoundButton_TC = "43fc13" -- GUID of object used for round button (example: Objects -> Components -> Go -> Black Piece)
	gStartMarker_TC = "20075b" -- GUID of object used as the start player marker (example: Objects -> Components -> Chess -> Wood -> King Light)
    gTurnMarker_TC = "8d6851" -- GUID of object used as the turn marker (example: Objects -> Components -> Chess -> Wood -> Pawn Light)    

    -- OBJECT

    -- TABLE

    tTurnOrder_TC = {} -- Indexed table of player colors (ex. "Red") used to change the turn order for the next round

    -- VARIABLE

    -- URL of your custom image for the setup board (image size of 1024 x 1024 or greater recommended)
    vSetupBoardURL_TC = "https://steamusercontent-a.akamaihd.net/ugc/15382765005103720235/9722C6DCC0C267783BD130A12E6A77A00DF3CE4E/"

    -- INTEGER

	-- BOOLEAN

	bBoardOption1_TC = false
	bBoardOption2_TC = false

end

--[[ EVENTS ]]--

-- Triggered when a player chooses/changes color (returns "Grey" upon disconnect)
function onPlayerChangeColor(vPlayerColor)
    TC.onChangeColor(vPlayerColor) -- DO NOT DELETE! Table-related player tasks

    -- Enter code here

end

-- Triggered when on object leaves a container

    -- Table-related tasks if setup not yet triggered
