--[[ BUTTON FUNCTIONS ]]--

-- Table control panel button
function click_toggleControl_TC(_, vPlayerColor, bAltClick)
    if not bAltClick then
        if Util.perm(vPlayerColor) then
            if not bPanelActive_TC then
                -- Open panel
                oPanel_TC.clearButtons()
                bPanelActive_TC = true
                tc.createButtons("Panel")
                tc.createInputs("Image")
                tc.createButtons("Image")
                tc.createInputs("Options")
                tc.createButtons("Options")
            else
                -- Close panel
                oPanel_TC.clearButtons()
                oPanel_TC.clearInputs()
                bPanelActive_TC = false
                tc.createButtons("Panel")
            end
        else
            Player[vPlayerColor].showInfoDialog("Host/promoted players only!")
        end
    end
end

-- Apply image button
function click_applyImage_TC(_, vPlayerColor, bAltClick)
    if not bAltClick then
        if Util.perm(vPlayerColor) then
            tc.applyImage(oPanel_TC.getInputs()[2].value)
            broadcastToAll("New image applied!", {0.2, 0.9, 0.2})
        end
    end
end

-- Save image button
function click_saveImage_TC(_, vPlayerColor, bAltClick)
    if not bAltClick then
        if Util.perm(vPlayerColor) then
            local vName = oPanel_TC.getInputs()[1].value
            local vURL = oPanel_TC.getInputs()[2].value
            local nImageWidth = tonumber(oPanel_TC.getInputs()[3].value)
            local nImageHeight = tonumber(oPanel_TC.getInputs()[4].value)

            if not Util.is(vName) then
                broadcastToAll("Please supply a name for this save", {0.9, 0.2, 0.2})
            elseif not nImageWidth or not nImageHeight then
                broadcastToAll("Please supply valid image dimensions", {0.9, 0.2, 0.2})
            else
                if tc.imageIndex(vURL, vName) == nil then
                    table.insert(tImageData_TC, {
                        url = vURL,
                        name = vName,
                        width = nImageWidth,
                        height = nImageHeight,
                    })
                    broadcastToAll("Image URL and dimensions saved!", {0.2, 0.9, 0.2})

                    -- Refresh buttons
                    oPanel_TC.clearButtons()
                    tc.createButtons("Panel")
                    tc.createButtons("Image")
                    tc.createButtons("Options")
                else
                    broadcastToAll("There is already a save with this name and/or URL (ie. delete it first)", {0.9, 0.2, 0.2})
                end
            end
        end
    end
end

-- Saved images buttons
function click_loadSave_TC(_, vPlayerColor, bAltClick, nIndex)
    local tEntry = tImageData_TC[nIndex]
    if not tEntry then return end

    if not bAltClick then
        if Util.perm(vPlayerColor) then

            -- X-Wide (5x0) - 10P
            if nIndex == 1 then
                local tZoneColors = {"Blue","Green","Pink","Purple","Teal","Brown","Orange","White","Yellow","Red"}
                tc.applyConfig(vPlayerColor, tZoneColors, "XWide10", nIndex, 6)
                return
            end

            -- X-Tall (5x0) - 10P
            if nIndex == 2 then
                local tZoneColors = {"Blue","Green","Pink","Purple","Teal","Brown","Orange","White","Yellow","Red"}
                tc.applyConfig(vPlayerColor, tZoneColors, "XTall10", nIndex, 3.75)
                return
            end

            -- Wide (4x1) - 10P
            if nIndex == 3 then
                local tZoneColors = {"Blue","Green","Pink","Purple","Teal","Brown","Orange","White","Yellow","Red"}
                tc.applyConfig(vPlayerColor, tZoneColors, "Wide10", nIndex, 5)
                return
            end

            -- Tall (4x0) - 8P
            if nIndex == 4 then
                local tZoneColors = {"Blue","Green","Purple","Teal","Brown","Orange","Yellow","Red"}
                tc.applyConfig(vPlayerColor, tZoneColors, "Tall8", nIndex, 3.125)
                return
            end

            -- Full (3x2) - 10P
            if nIndex == 5 then
                local tZoneColors = {"Blue","Green","Pink","Purple","Teal","Brown","Orange","White","Yellow","Red"}
                tc.applyConfig(vPlayerColor, tZoneColors, "Full10", nIndex, 4)
                return
            end

            -- Square (2x2) - 8P
            if nIndex == 6 then
                local tZoneColors = {"Blue","Green","Pink","Purple","Orange","White","Yellow","Red"}
                tc.applyConfig(vPlayerColor, tZoneColors, "Square8", nIndex, 3)
                return
            end

            -- Square (1x1) - 4P
            if nIndex == 7 then
                local tZoneColors = {"Blue","Green","Yellow","Red"}
                tc.applyConfig(vPlayerColor, tZoneColors, "Square4", nIndex, 2)
                return
            end

            -- editInput uses indices starting at 0
            oPanel_TC.editInput({index=0, value=tEntry.name})
            oPanel_TC.editInput({index=1, value=tEntry.url})
            oPanel_TC.editInput({index=2, value=tostring(tEntry.width or "")})
            oPanel_TC.editInput({index=3, value=tostring(tEntry.height or "")})

            -- Uncheck grid image checkbox
            if tTableData_TC.grid then
                local fFindButton = function(obj) return obj.click_function == "check_applyGrid_TC" end
                local oButton = Util.find(oPanel_TC, fFindButton)
                if oButton then oPanel_TC.editButton({index=oButton.index, label=""}) end
                tTableData_TC.grid = false
            end

            tc.applyImage(oPanel_TC.getInputs()[2].value)
            broadcastToAll("Saved image applied!", {0.2, 0.9, 0.2})
        end
    else
        if nIndex <= 7 then
            Notes.setNotes(tEntry.name..":\n"..tGridURLs_TC[nIndex].."\n")
        end
    end
end

-- Delete buttons
function click_deleteSave_TC(_, vPlayerColor, nIndex)
    if not bAltClick then
        if Util.perm(vPlayerColor) then
            table.remove(tImageData_TC, nIndex)
            oPanel_TC.clearButtons()
            tc.createButtons("Panel")
            tc.createButtons("Image")
            tc.createButtons("Options")
            broadcastToAll("Saved image removed!", {0.2, 0.9, 0.2})
        end
    end
end

function tc.createInputs(vType)
    local nConfigIndex = tc.configIndex(tTableData_TC.config)

    local tInput = { -- Default input parameters
        function_owner = Global,
        input_function = "none",
        label = "",
        value = "",
        tooltip = "",
        width = 4000,
        height = 224,
        color = "White",
        font_size = 200,
        font_color = {0, 0, 0},
        position = {0, 0, 0},
        rotation = {0, 0, 0},
        scale = {1, 1, 1},
        alignment = 3,
        validation = 1,
        tab = 2,
    }

    if vType == "Image" then
        local vName = ""
        local vURL = oSurface_TC.getCustomObject().diffuse

        if Util.is(tc.imageIndex(vURL)) then
            vName = tImageData_TC[tc.imageIndex(vURL)].name
        else
            vName = tImageData_TC[nConfigIndex].name
        end

        -- Name input (index 0)
        tInput.label = "Name"
        tInput.value = vName
        tInput.tooltip = "Enter name of image (only used for saving)"
        tInput.position = {0, 0, 2}
        oPanel_TC.createInput(tInput)

        -- URL input (index 1)
        tInput.label = "URL"
        tInput.value = vURL
        tInput.tooltip = "Enter URL of image"
        tInput.position = {0, 0, 3}
        oPanel_TC.createInput(tInput)
    end

    if vType == "Options" then
        -- Prefill scale and try to prefill dimensions if current surface matches a saved entry
        local nScaleWidth = oRailTop_TC.getScale().x
        local nScaleDepth = oRailLeft_TC.getScale().x
        local vURL = oSurface_TC.getCustomObject().diffuse
        local nImageWidth, nImageHeight = "", ""
        local nIndex = tc.imageIndex(vURL)

        if Util.is(nIndex) then
            nImageWidth = tImageData_TC[nIndex].width or ""
            nImageHeight = tImageData_TC[nIndex].height or ""
        else
            nImageWidth = tImageData_TC[nConfigIndex].width or ""
            nImageHeight = tImageData_TC[nConfigIndex].height or ""
        end

        -- Dimension width input (index 2)
        tInput.input_function = "input_imgWidth_TC"
        tInput.label = "Width"
        tInput.value = tostring(nImageWidth)
        tInput.tooltip = "Image width in pixels"
        tInput.width = 800
        tInput.position = {7, 0, 2}
        oPanel_TC.createInput(tInput)

        -- Dimension height input (index 3)
        tInput.input_function = "input_imgHeight_TC"
        tInput.label = "Height"
        tInput.value = tostring(nImageHeight)
        tInput.tooltip = "Image height in pixels"
        tInput.position = {9, 0, 2}
        oPanel_TC.createInput(tInput)

        -- Scale width input (index 4)
        tInput.input_function = "input_scaleWidth_TC"
        tInput.label = string.char(8644)
        tInput.value = nScaleWidth
        tInput.tooltip = "Scale width"
        tInput.width = 400
        tInput.position = {7.5, 0, 6.15}
        oPanel_TC.createInput(tInput)

        -- Scale depth input (index 5)
        tInput.input_function = "input_scaleDepth_TC"
        tInput.label = string.char(8645)
        tInput.value = nScaleDepth
        tInput.tooltip = "Scale depth"
        tInput.position = {8.5, 0, 6.15}
        oPanel_TC.createInput(tInput)
    end
end

__bundle_register("lib.Util", function(require, _LOADED, __bundle_register, __bundle_modules)
local util = {}  -- Library of functions for utilities lua module

--[[ UTILITIES ]]--

-- GENERAL

function util.is(vValue) -- Return false if object is nil, table is empty or variable has no value
    if vValue ~= nil then
        if type(vValue) == "string" or type(vValue) == "number" then
            return vValue ~= ""
        elseif type(vValue) == "table" then
            return next(vValue) ~= nil
        else
            return true
        end
    else
        return false
    end
end

function util.pause(nTime) -- Time delay in seconds (execute only in a coroutine)
    if util.is(nTime) then
        local time = os.clock() + nTime
        while os.clock() < time do coroutine.yield(0) end
    else
        coroutine.yield(0)
    end
end

function util.perm(vPlayerColor) -- Return true if player color is either host or promoted
    if Player[vPlayerColor].host == true or Player[vPlayerColor].promoted == true then
        return true
    else
        return false
    end
end

function none() end -- Dummy function (absorbs unwanted triggers)

-- OBJECT

function util.get(gObj) -- Returns object from GUID
	return getObjectFromGUID(gObj)
end

function util.del(vObj) -- Delete object (GUID or object)
    if util.get(vObj) then
        util.get(vObj).destruct()
    else
        vObj.destruct()
    end
end

function util.lock(obj) -- Lock object (when resting)
    local fCheckIfResting = function() return obj.resting end
    local fSetLock = function() obj.setLock(true) end
    Wait.condition(fSetLock, fCheckIfResting, 10)
end

function util.take(tArgs) -- Returns a reference to the object taken from the container to a position based on the specified parameters
    --[[ IMPORTANT! Pass arguments to this function as a table (see below)
    Util.take({
        Container = <reference -or- GUID>, (REQUIRED); ex. oDeck -or- "123456" -> container where object is taken from
        Pos = <position>, (DEFAULT: container's position +2 on x-axis); ex. {-3.75, 3.00, -9.75} -> position to place object
            Note: x = left-/right+, y = up+/down-, z = back+/front-
        Rot = <boolean -or- rotation>, (DEFAULT: container's rotation); ex. decks only: (true -> flip -or- false -> not flipped) -or- {90, 180, 0} -> rotation of taken object
            Note: x = pitch (nodding head), y = yaw (shaking head), z = roll (tilting head)
        Top = <boolean -or- integer -or- GUID>, (DEFAULT: true); ex. true -> take from top -or- false -> take from bottom -or- 0 -> 1st obj in container -or- "123456" -> guid of obj in container
        Smooth = <boolean>, (DEFAULT: true); ex. true -> move smoothly -or- false -> move instantly
        Call = <function>, (DEFAULT: nil); ex. function(obj) cbTake(obj) end -> function called after object is fully spawned
    })
    ]]--
    -- Default parameters
    local tParams = { -- DO NOT EDIT
        position = (tArgs.Pos or nil), -- position to place object
        flip = nil, -- flip card? (decks only)
        rotation = nil, -- rotation of taken object
        top = nil, -- take from top or bottom?
        index = nil, -- index of object in container (starts at 0)
        guid = nil, -- GUID of object in container (see Deck GUID Fixer)
        smooth = true, -- move smoothly or instantly?
        callback_function = tArgs.Call, -- function called after object is fully spawned
    }
    -- Object
    local oContainer = nil
    if util.get(tArgs.Container) then
        oContainer = util.get(tArgs.Container)
    else
        oContainer = tArgs.Container
    end
    -- Flip/rotation
    if util.is(tArgs.Rot) then
        if type(tArgs.Rot) == "boolean" then
            tParams.flip = tArgs.Rot
        else
            if type(tArgs.Rot) == "table" then tParams.rotation = tArgs.Rot end
        end
    end
    -- Top/index/GUID
    if util.is(tArgs.Top) then
        if type(tArgs.Top) == "boolean" then
            tParams.top = tArgs.Top
        else
            if #tostring(tArgs.Top) == 6 then
                tParams.guid = tArgs.Top
            else
                if util.is(tonumber(tArgs.Top, 10)) then tParams.index = tonumber(tArgs.Top, 10) end
            end
        end
    end
    --Smooth
    if util.is(tArgs.Smooth) then tParams.smooth = tArgs.Smooth end
    return oContainer.takeObject(tParams)
end

function util.move(tArgs) -- Moves an existing object to a position based on the specified parameters
    --[[ IMPORTANT! Pass arguments to this function as a table (see below)
    Util.move({
        Obj = <reference -or- GUID>, (REQUIRED); ex. oToken -or- "123456" -> object to be moved
        Pos = <position>, (REQUIRED); ex. {-3.75, 3.00, -9.75} -> position to move object to
            Note: x = left-/right+, y = up+/down-, z = back+/front-
        Rot = <rotation>, (DEFAULT: {0, 0, 0}); ex. {90, 180, 0} -> rotation of moved object
            Note: x = pitch (nodding head), y = yaw (shaking head), z = roll (tilting head) -> -180 to 180 degrees
        Smooth = <boolean>, (DEFAULT: false); ex. true -> move smoothly -or- false -> move instantly
        Fast = <boolean>, (DEFAULT: false); ex. true -> move fast (requires Smooth = true)
        Collide = <boolean>, (DEFAULT: false); ex. true -> collide while moving (requires Smooth = true)
    })
    ]]--
    -- Object
    local oMove = nil
    if util.get(tArgs.Obj) then
        oMove = util.get(tArgs.Obj)
    else
        oMove = tArgs.Obj
    end
    -- Smooth
    local bSmooth = tArgs.Smooth
    if tArgs.Fast or tArgs.Collide then bSmooth = true end
    -- Position
    if bSmooth then
        oMove.setPositionSmooth(util.toVector(tArgs.Pos), tArgs.Collide, tArgs.Fast)
        if util.is(tArgs.Rot) then oMove.setRotationSmooth(util.toVector(tArgs.Rot), tArgs.Collide, tArgs.Fast) end
    else
        oMove.setPosition(util.toVector(tArgs.Pos))
        if util.is(tArgs.Rot) then oMove.setRotation(util.toVector(tArgs.Rot)) end
    end
end

function util.spawn(tArgs) -- Returns a reference to the spawned object using specified parameters
    --[[ IMPORTANT! Pass arguments to this function as a table (see below)
    Util.spawn({
        Type = <spawnable object type -or- object data table>, (REQUIRED); ex. "Custom_Board"
            Spawnable object types: https://api.tabletopsimulator.com/built-in-object/#spawnable-names
        Pos = <position>, (DEFAULT: {0, 2, 0}); ex. {-3.75, 3.00, -9.75} -> position of spawned object
            Note: x = left-/right+, y = up+/down-, z = back+/front-
        Rot = <rotation>, (DEFAULT: {0, 0, 0}); ex. {90, 180, 0} -> rotation of spawned object
            Note: x = pitch (nodding head), y = yaw (shaking head), z = roll (tilting head) -> -180 to 180 degrees
        Sca = <scale>, (DEFAULT: {1, 1, 1}); ex. {4, 0.5, 2} -> scale of spawned object
            Note: x = width (left/right), y = height (up/down), z = length (back/front)
        Snap = <boolean>, (DEFAULT: false) -> snap to grid?
        Sound = <boolean>, (DEFAULT: false) -> play sound when object spawns?
        Call = <function>, (DEFAULT: nil); ex. function(obj) cbSpawn(obj) end -> function called after object is fully spawned
    })
    ]]--
    -- Default parameters
    local tParams = { -- DO NOT EDIT
        --type = nil, -- name of spawnable object
        --data = nil, -- object data table (accepts table from getData)
        position = (tArgs.Pos or {0, 2, 0}), -- position of spawned object
        rotation = (tArgs.Rot or {0, 0, 0}), -- rotation of spawned object
        scale = (tArgs.Sca or {1, 1, 1}), -- scale of spawned object
        --snap_to_grid = nil, -- snap to grid?
        --sound = nil, -- play sound when object spawns?
        callback_function = tArgs.Call, -- function called after object is fully spawned
    }
    -- Argument type
    if type(tArgs.Type) == "table" then
        tParams.data = tArgs.Type
        return spawnObjectData(tParams)
    else
        tParams.type = tArgs.Type
        tParams.snap_to_grid = tArgs.Snap
        tParams.sound = tArgs.Sound
        return spawnObject(tParams)
    end
end

function util.cast(tArgs) -- Returns an indexed table of filtered objects within position's radius
    --[[ IMPORTANT! Pass arguments to this function as a table (see below)
    Util.cast({
        Type = <integer>, (DEFAULT: 2); 1 = ray, 2 = sphere, 3 = box -> type of cast
        Pos = <position>, (DEFAULT: {0, 1, 0}); ex. {-3.75, 3.00, -9.75} -> starting position of cast
            Note: x = left-/right+, y = up+/down-, z = back+/front-
        Radius = <integer -or- scale>, (DEFAULT: {1, 1, 1}); ex. 2 same as {2, 2, 2} -> scale of cast shape (sphere/box only)
            Note: x = width (left/right), y = height (up/down), z = length (back/front)
        Direction = <direction>, (DEFAULT: {0, 1, 0}); ex. {0, 1, 0} -> direction of cast
            Note: x = left-/right+, y = up+/down-, z = back+/front- -> -1 to 1
        Rot = <rotation>, (DEFAULT: {0, 0, 0}); ex. {90, 180, 0} -> rotation of cast shape (box only)
        Dist = <integer>, (DEFAULT: 0); ex. 0 = infinity -> maximum distance cast will travel (ray only)
            Note: x = pitch (nodding head), y = yaw (shaking head), z = roll (tilting head) -> -180 to 180 degrees
        Filter = <function>, (DEFAULT: nil); ex. function(obj) return obj.type == "Deck" end -> returns objects that only match criteria
            Note: Use pre-defined filter function names instead (see below); ex. "isCardOrDeck"
        Debug = <boolean>, (DEFAULT: false) -> visualize cast for user?
    })
    ]]--
    -- Default parameters
    local tParams = { -- DO NOT EDIT
        type = (tArgs.Type or 2), -- type of cast (1 = ray, 2 = sphere, 3 = box)
        origin = (tArgs.Pos or {0, 1, 0}), -- starting position of cast
        size = {1, 1, 1}, -- scale of cast shape (sphere/box only)
        direction = (tArgs.Direction or {0, 1, 0}), -- direction of cast
        max_distance = (tArgs.Dist or 0), -- maximum distance cast will travel (ray only)
        orientation = nil, -- rotation of cast shape (box only)
        debug = tArgs.Debug, -- visualize cast for user?
    }
    -- Use pre-defined filter function names as argument (instead of function)
    local fFilter = nil
    local tFilterFunc = {
        isCard = function(x) return x.type == "Card" end,
        isDeck = function(x) return x.type == "Deck" end,
        isCardOrDeck = function(x) return x.type == "Card" or x.type == "Deck" end,
        isTileOrToken = function(x) return not x.Book and (x.type == "Tile" or x.type == "Generic") end,
        isInteractable = function(x) return x.interactable end
    }
    if type(tArgs.Filter) == "string" then
        fFilter = tFilterFunc[tArgs.Filter]
    elseif type(tArgs.Filter) == "function" then
        fFilter = tArgs.Filter
    end
    -- Radius/scale
    if tArgs.Type ~= 1 then -- No scale for ray cast 
        if type(tArgs.Radius) == "table" then
            tParams.size = tArgs.Radius
        else
            local nRadius = tArgs.Radius
            if type(nRadius) == "number" then
                tParams.size = {nRadius, nRadius, nRadius}
            end
        end
    end
    -- Orientation
    if tArgs.Type == 3 then -- Rotation for box cast only
        if type(tArgs.Rot) == "table" then tParams.orientation = tArgs.Rot end
    end
    -- Objects from cast
    local tObjects = {}
    for _, tArr in ipairs(Physics.cast(tParams)) do
        if (not fFilter or fFilter(tArr.hit_object)) then table.insert(tObjects, tArr.hit_object) end
    end
    return tObjects
end

-- TABLE

function util.isArray(tTable) -- Returns true if table is an array (ie. indexed table) or empty
    if type(tTable) ~= "table" then
        print("<Util.isArray>: bad argument (table expected, got "..type(tTable)..")")
        return nil
    end
    -- Check if keys are numerical as well as count them
    local nCount = 0
    for k,v in pairs(tTable) do
        if type(k) ~= "number" then return false else nCount = nCount+1 end
    end
    -- Keys are numerical, check if sequential & starting with 1
    for i = 1, nCount do
        -- Also check type if value is nil
        if not tTable[i] and type(tTable[i]) ~= "nil" then return false end
    end
    return true
end

function util.copyTable(tTable) -- Returns a copy of a table
	-- Note: Lua's assignment operator (ex. tTable1 = tTable2) is by reference &, therefore, doesn't create a copy of the table
    -- ie. any changes to tTable1 also apply to tTable2 (& vice versa) as they are equal by reference (not copies of each other)
	local tCopy = {}
	for k, v in pairs(tTable) do
		tCopy[k] = v
	end
	return tCopy
end

function util.randomizeTable(tTable) -- Returns a table of randomized entries (from an indexed table only)
    local tRandomized = {}
    for i, v in ipairs(tTable) do tRandomized[i] = v end
    for i = #tRandomized, 1, -1 do
        local nRnd = math.random(i)
        tRandomized[i], tRandomized[nRnd] = tRandomized[nRnd], tRandomized[i]
    end
    return tRandomized
end

function util.invertTable(tTable, bPivot) -- Returns a table of inverted (reversed) entries (from an indexed table only)
    -- bPivot = <boolean>, (DEFAULT: false) ex. true -> reverse table direction, not entries (see examples below)
    -- Example1: bPivot = false -> {"Red", "Green", "Blue"} = {"Blue", "Green", "Red"} (ie. table flipped)
    -- Example2: bPivot = true -> {"Red", "Green", "Blue"} = {"Red", "Blue", "Green"} (ie. from clockwise to counter-clockwise)
    local tReversed = {}
    local nEntries = #tTable
    for i, v in ipairs(tTable) do
        if bPivot then
            if i == 1 then
                tReversed[i] = v
            else
                tReversed[nEntries+2 - i] = v
            end
        else
            tReversed[nEntries+1 - i] = v
        end
    end
    return tReversed
end

function util.removeDuplicates(tTable) -- Returns a table with duplicate entries removed (from an indexed table only)
    local tFixed = {}
    local tDetect = {}
    for _, vValue in ipairs(tTable) do
        if not tDetect[vValue] then tFixed[#tFixed+1] = vValue end
        tDetect[vValue] = true
    end
    return tFixed
end

function util.filterTable(tTable, fFunc) -- Returns a table of filtered entries where a filter function returns true
    local tResult = {}
    if util.isArray(tTable) then
        for i = 1, #tTable do
            if fFunc(tTable[i]) then tResult[#tResult+1] = tTable[i] end
        end
    else
        for k, v in pairs(tTable) do
            if fFunc(k) then tResult[k] = v end
        end
    end
    return tResult
end

-- BUTTON

function util.find(obj, fFindButton) -- Returns a reference to a button where the filter function returns true
    --[[ Example (see below): Code that updates a specific button's label (without an index) using this function
    local oAttached = Util.get("abc123") -> Get reference for object that button is attached to
    local fFilter = function(obj) return obj.click_function == "click_doIt" end -> Filter function that finds button based on its click_function
    local oButton = Util.find(oAttached, fFilter) -> Get reference for button by passing button's object & the filter function
    if oButton then oAttached.editButton({index = oButton.index, label = "Hello world!"}) end -> Update button's label if button found
    ]]--
    if not util.is(fFindButton) then error("Util.find: No function supplied") end
    for _, oButton in ipairs(obj.getButtons()) do
        if fFindButton(oButton) then return oButton end
    end
    return nil
end

-- VECTOR

-- See NUMBER -> "util.round" to round a vector position

function util.toVector(tVector) -- Returns a vector (XYZ-formatted) from a table (position, rotation or direction)
    -- Note: For vector math, tables of position, rotation or direction must be converted to vectors
    -- tVector = <table/vector>, (REQUIRED); ex. {6.25, 1.1, -9.75}
    -- Note: x = left-/right+, y = up+/down-, z = back+/front-    
    if type(tVector) == "table" then
        if util.isArray(tVector) then
            -- Indexed
            local tVector1 = {x=0, y=0, z=0}
            tVector1.x = tVector[1]
            tVector1.y = tVector[2]
            tVector1.z = tVector[3]
            return Vector(tVector1)
        else
            -- XYZ-formatted
            return Vector(tVector)
        end
    end
    return tVector
end

function util.isVectorEqual(tVector1, tVector2) -- Returns true if vectors (position, rotation or direction) are very similar to each other
    -- Note: Returns true if there is less than ~0.03 difference in magnitude (use a vector magnitude calculator to calculate magnitude)
    -- tVector1 = <table/vector>, (REQUIRED); ex. {1, 2, 3} -> considered equal to tVector2 example (0.0206 difference in magnitude)
    -- tVector2 = <table/vector>, (REQUIRED); ex. {0.987, 1.987, 2.987}
    -- Note: x = left-/right+, y = up+/down-, z = back+/front-
    return util.toVector(tVector1) == util.toVector(tVector2)
end

function util.inVectorRange(tVector1, tVector2, nRange) -- Returns true if vectors (position only) are within range of each other
    -- Note: Use line tool (set as Grid, 3D, Auto, Center) to measure distance between vectors (positions)
    -- tVector1 = <table/vector>, (REQUIRED); ex. {1, 2, 3} -> considered in range of tVector2 if nRange = 3.32
    -- tVector2 = <table/vector>, (REQUIRED); ex. {4, 3, 2}
    -- Note: x = left-/right+, y = up+/down-, z = back+/front-
    -- nRange = <number>, (REQUIRED); ex. 3.32 -> returns true if distance between vectors doesn't exceed 3.32
    local nDist = Vector.distance(util.toVector(tVector1), util.toVector(tVector2))
    return nDist <= nRange
end

-- NUMBER

function util.round(nNumber, nDecimals, bFloor) -- Returns rounded number or position
    -- Note: If you use bFloor, you'll need to provide nDecimals
    -- nNumber = <decimal number>, (REQUIRED) ex. 3.4567 -> number to be rounded
    -- nDecimals = <integer>, (DEFAULT: 2) -> number of decimal places to round number
    -- bFloor = <boolean>, (DEFAULT: false) ex. true -> always round number down
    local nMultiple = 10^(nDecimals or 2)
    local nFactor = 0.5
    if bFloor then nFactor = 0 end
    -- Position
    if type(nNumber) == "table" then
        local tNumber = {}
        if util.isArray(nNumber) then
            -- Indexed
            for i = 1, 3 do tNumber[i] = math.floor(nNumber[i] * nMultiple + 0.5) / nMultiple end
        else
            -- XYZ-formatted
            for k, v in pairs(nNumber) do
                tNumber[k] = math.floor(v * nMultiple + 0.5) / nMultiple
            end
        end
        return Vector(tNumber)
    else
        -- Number
        return math.floor(nNumber * nMultiple + nFactor) / nMultiple
    end
end

function util.inNumRange(nNum1, nNum2, nRange) -- Returns true if numbers are within range of each other
    -- nNum1 = <number>, (REQUIRED); ex. -5 -> considered in range of nNum2 if nRange = 10
    -- nNum2 = <number>, (REQUIRED); ex. 5
    -- nRange = <number>, (REQUIRED); ex. 10 -> returns true if distance between numbers doesn't exceed 10 
    local bReturn = true
    if nNum1 >= 0 then -- Positive
        if nNum1 - nRange > nNum2 or nNum1 + nRange < nNum2 then bReturn = false end
    end
    if nNum1 < 0 then -- Negative
        if nNum1 - nRange > nNum2 or nNum1 + nRange < nNum2 then bReturn = false end
    end
    return bReturn
end

-- COLOR

function util.toColorVector(vColor, bAlpha, nAlpha) -- Returns a color vector (RGBA) from a color string (ex. "Red") or table/vector
    -- vColor = <color string -or- table/vector>, (REQUIRED); ex. "Red" -or- {0.856, 0.1, 0.094}
    -- bAlpha = <boolean>, (DEFAULT: false); ex. true -> include alpha (transparency)
    -- nAlpha = <number>, (DEFAULT: nil); ex. 0.5 -> set alpha to 50% transparency (decimal number between 0-1)
    -- Note: r = red, g = green, b = blue, a = alpha (transparency)
    if type(vColor) == "table" then
        if util.isArray(vColor) then
            -- Indexed
            local tColor = {r=0, g=0, b=0, a=1}
            tColor.r = vColor[1]
            tColor.g = vColor[2]
            tColor.b = vColor[3]
            if bAlpha then
                if util.is(nAlpha) then
                    tColor.a = nAlpha
                else
                    if util.is(vColor[4]) then tColor.a = vColor[4] end
                end
            end
            return Color(tColor)
        else
            -- RGBA-formatted
            if bAlpha then
                if util.is(nAlpha) then vColor.a = nAlpha end
            else
                vColor.a = 1
            end
            return Color(vColor)
        end
    else
        -- Player color
        local tColorList = Color.list
        for _, vColor1 in ipairs(tColorList) do
            if vColor == vColor1 then
                local tColor = stringColorToRGB(vColor)
                ---@diagnostic disable-next-line: inject-field
                if bAlpha and util.is(nAlpha) then tColor.a = nAlpha end
                return tColor
            end
        end
    end
    return vColor
end

function util.toColorString(tColor) -- Returns a color string (ex. "Red") from a color (table/vector)
    -- tColor = <table/vector>, (REQUIRED); ex. {0.118, 0.53, 1} -> ignores alpha (transparency)
    -- Note: r = red, g = green, b = blue, a = alpha (transparency)            
    return Color.toString(util.toColorVector(tColor))
end

function util.toColorHex(vColor, bAlpha, nAlpha) -- Returns a hex color string (ex. #da1917) from a color string (ex. "Red") or table/vector
    -- vColor = <color string -or- table/vector>, (REQUIRED); ex. "Red" -or- {0.856, 0.1, 0.094}
    -- bAlpha = <boolean>, (DEFAULT: false); ex. true -> include alpha (transparency)
    -- nAlpha = <number>, (DEFAULT: nil); ex. 0.5 -> set alpha to 50% transparency (decimal number between 0-1)    
    -- Note: r = red, g = green, b = blue, a = alpha (transparency)
    local tColor = util.toColorVector(vColor, bAlpha, nAlpha)
    return "#"..tColor:toHex(bAlpha)
end

function util.isColorEqual(vColor1, vColor2, bAlpha) -- Returns true if any combo of color string (ex. "Red") or color (table/vector) is equal
    -- vColor1 = <color string -or- table/vector>, (REQUIRED); ex. "Red" -or- {0.856, 0.1, 0.094}
    -- vColor2 = <color string -or- table/vector>, (REQUIRED)
    -- bAlpha = <boolean>, (DEFAULT: false); ex. false -> ignore alpha (transparency) [a = 1]
    -- Note: r = red, g = green, b = blue, a = alpha (transparency)        
    return util.toColorVector(vColor1, bAlpha) == util.toColorVector(vColor2, bAlpha)
end

-- 3D TEXT

function util.text(tArgs) -- Returns a reference to the spawned text using specified parameters
    --[[ IMPORTANT! Pass arguments to this function as a table (see below)
    Util.text({
        Text = <variable>, (REQUIRED); ex. "Hello world!" -> 3D text to be spawned
        Pos = <position>, (DEFAULT: {0, 2, 0}); ex. {-3.75, 3.00, -9.75} -> position for spawned text
            Note: x = left-/right+, y = up+/down-, z = back+/front-
        Rot = <rotation>, (DEFAULT: {90, 0, 0}); ex. {90, 180, 0} -> rotation of spawned text
            Note: x = pitch (nodding head), y = yaw (shaking head), z = roll (tilting head) -> -180 to 180 degrees
        Sca = <scale>, (DEFAULT: {1, 1, 1}); ex. {4, 0.5, 2} -> scale of spawned text
            Note: x = width (left/right), y = height (up/down), z = length (back/front)
        Color = <color string -or- vector>, (DEFAULT: "White"); ex. "Green" -or- {0.192, 0.701, 0.168} -> font color of spawned text
            Note: r = red, g = green, b = blue, a = alpha (transparency)
        Size = <integer>, (DEFAULT: 64); ex. 12 -> font size of spawned text
    })
    ]]--
    -- Callback function
    local function cbSpawnText(oText, tSca)
        if util.is(tSca) then oText.setScale(tSca) end
    end
    -- Spawn text
    local oText = util.spawn({
        Type = "3DText", -- 3D text to be spawned
        Pos = (tArgs.Pos or {0, 2, 0}), -- position for spawned text
        Rot = (tArgs.Rot or {90, 0, 0}), -- rotation of spawned text (flat)
        Call = function(obj) cbSpawnText(obj, tArgs.Sca) end
    })
    -- Set text properties
    oText.TextTool.setValue(tArgs.Text)
    if util.is(tArgs.Color) then oText.TextTool.setFontColor(tArgs.Color) end
    if util.is(tArgs.Size) then oText.TextTool.setFontSize(tArgs.Size) end
    return oText
end

-- NOTIFY

function util.broadcast(vText, vPlayerColor, vColor) -- Broadcast message to player color(s)
    -- vText = <variable>, (REQUIRED); ex. "Hello world!" -> text broadcast to player(s)
    -- vPlayerColor = <color string -or- "All">, (REQUIRED); ex. "Red" -> broadcast to red player color -or- "All" -> broadcast to all player colors
    -- vColor = <color string>, (DEFAULT: nil), ex. "Red" -> broadcast in specified color
	if vPlayerColor == "All" then
        if vColor == nil then
            broadcastToAll(vText)
        else
            broadcastToAll(vText, vColor)
        end
    else
        --if Player[vPlayerColor].seated then
            if vColor == nil then
                broadcastToColor(vText, vPlayerColor, vPlayerColor)
            else
                broadcastToColor(vText, vPlayerColor, vColor)
            end
        --end
    end
end

function util.send(vText, vPlayerColor, vColor) -- Send message to player color(s)
    -- vText = <variable>, (REQUIRED); ex. "Hello world!" -> text broadcast to player(s)
    -- vPlayerColor = <color string -or- "All">, (REQUIRED); ex. "Red" -> broadcast to red player color -or- "All" -> broadcast to all player colors
    -- vColor = <color string>, (DEFAULT: nil), ex. "Red" -> broadcast in specified color
	if vPlayerColor == "All" then
        if not util.is(vColor) then
            printToAll(vText)
        else
            printToAll(vText, vColor)
        end
    else
        if Player[vPlayerColor].seated then
            if not util.is(vColor) then
                printToColor(vText, vPlayerColor, vPlayerColor)
            else
                printToColor(vText, vPlayerColor, vColor)
            end
        end
    end
end

-- CAMERA

function util.look(tArgs) -- Set player camera to indicated position
    --[[ IMPORTANT! Pass arguments to this function as a table (see below)
    Util.look({
        Color = <color string -or "All">, (REQUIRED); ex. "Red" -or- "All" -> change camera for player color(s) (forcing 3rd person)
        Pos = <position>, (DEFAULT: {0, 1, 0}); ex. {-3.75, 3.00, -9.75} -> camera position to look at
            Note: x = left-/right+, y = up+/down-, z = back+/front-
        Dist = <integer>, (DEFAULT: 40) -> camera distance from the position
        Pitch = <integer>, (DEFAULT: 0) -> pitch angle of camera (nodding head; 0 to 90 degrees)
        Yaw = <integer>, (DEFAULT: 0) -> yaw angle of camera (shaking head; 0 to 360 degrees)
    })
    ]]--
    -- Default parameters
	local tParams = {
        position = (tArgs.Pos or {0, 1, 0}), -- camera position to look at
        distance = (tArgs.Dist or 40), -- camera distance from the position
        pitch = (tArgs.Pitch or 0), -- pitch angle of camera (nodding head; 0 to 90 degrees)
        yaw = (tArgs.Yaw or 0), -- yaw angle of camera (shaking head; -180 to 180 degrees)
    }
    -- Set player camera
    local tColors = util.seated()
    if tArgs.Color == "All" then
        for i, vColor in ipairs(tColors) do Player[vColor].lookAt(tParams) end
    else
        Player[tArgs.Color].lookAt(tParams)
    end
end

-- PLAYER

function util.getHostColor() -- Return host player's color
    for _, vColor in ipairs(Player.getColors()) do
        if vColor ~= "Grey" then
            if Player[vColor].host then return vColor end
        end
    end
end

function util.seated()  -- Returns an indexed table of seated player colors
    local tColors = {}
    for i, oPlayer in ipairs(Player.getPlayers()) do
        if oPlayer.color ~= "Grey" and oPlayer.color ~= "Black" then tColors[#tColors+1] = oPlayer.color end
    end
    return tColors
end

-- HTTP

---@param vURL table|string URL for the GET request
---@param fSuccess fun(vData: string) Function called when request successful
---@param fError fun(vError: string) Function called when error occurs (network or HTTP)
function util.getHTTP(vURL, fSuccess, fError) -- Perform http get request
    -- vURL = <variable>, (REQUIRED); ex. "https://api.github.com/zen" -> URL for the GET request
    -- fSuccess = <function>, (REQUIRED); ex. function(vData) broadcastToAll(vData) end -> Function called when request successful
    -- fError = <function>, (DEFAULT: nil); ex. function(vError) broadcastToAll(vError) end -> Function called when when error occurs (network or HTTP)
    -- Format URL if a table (ex. {"api", "data"} -> "api/data")
    if type(vURL) == "table" then vURL = table.concat(vURL, "/") end
    -- Perform http get request
    WebRequest.get(vURL,
        function(oRequest)
            if oRequest.is_error then
                -- Network error
                local vError = oRequest.error or "Unknown network error"
                if fError then fError(vError) end
                -- HTTP error (3xx - 5xx status codes)
                elseif oRequest.response_code and (oRequest.response_code < 200 and oRequest.response_code >= 300) then
                    local vError = "HTTP Error "..
                    oRequest.response_code..": "..(oRequest.text or "No error message provided.")
                    if fError then fError(vError) end
                else
                -- HTTP success (2xx status codes)
                if fSuccess then fSuccess(oRequest.text) end
            end
        end
    )
end

return util
end)
return __bundle_require("__root")"

title: Untitled
content:
“I will,” Atreus said. His distracted gaze went to a slate slab scrawled with runes adjacent to the path that led away. Curiosity drew him over for a closer look.
“Father, look at this,” he said excitedly, seizing upon another opportunity to demonstrate to his father what he knew.
“Read it to me.”
“It says a long time ago the Jötnar would gather here to trade with the gods. This place was a marketplace. Do you think Odin himself was ever here?”

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 6

title: Untitled
content:
“Do not move. I will get to you,” Kratos commanded, suppressing his anger so as to deliver his words with a calm demeanor, though terror ripped at his insides.
“I’m slipping!” Atreus cried out.
The structure gave way. As the bridge unraveled, Kratos launched himself for Atreus. While his son clung to his outstretched arm, Kratos curled his son’s body against his chest as they tumbled. Landing with a crushing thud, his son still atop him, Kratos endured the brunt of their twenty-foot fall.
“Anything broken?” Kratos queried, running hands along his son’s arms to check.
“I’m unhurt!” Atreus said, irritated by his father’s coddling.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 6

title: Untitled
content:
Anxious, Atreus waited as the draugr lifted their rusted swords into attack position. He fired to hit the closest one in the temple, while Kratos flung his axe in time to hit the other in its head. Both fell immediately.
“How was that?” Atreus asked.
“Better. But leave me enough space to fight,” Kratos insisted.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 6

title: Filter booking table result  by logged-id user id
content:
<?php 

add_filter( 'em_bookings_get_default_search', 'my_em_bookings_get_default_search', 100, 1 );
function my_em_bookings_get_default_search( $search_defaults ){
	if ( 
         ( isset($_REQUEST['person_id']) || $_REQUEST['action'] == 'em_bookings_table' ) 
         && !is_admin() 
         && !current_user_can( 'manage_options' ) 
       ){
		$current_user = wp_get_current_user();
		$search_defaults['owner']  = $current_user->ID;
		$search_defaults['person'] = $current_user->ID;
	}
	return $search_defaults;
}

title: Untitled
content:
Atreus tumbled away to escape the closest draugr, notching his arrow before firing it quickly, to take the first draugr down with a shot into its forehead. As the second charged Atreus, Kratos retreated from his attackers to fire his axe into the draugr’s neck from behind. The corpse faltered to its knees, staring vacantly at Atreus while he loaded another arrow, just in time to launch it into a headshot that dropped the draugr poised to jab its spear into his father.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 6

title: Untitled
content:
While Atreus fumbled for his next arrow, Kratos sliced the charging draugr with a wide swipe of his axe, severing it at waist height. Coming around in a backswing, he cleaved another draugr’s head from its rotting shoulders. The move provided Atreus with the time necessary to reload.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 6

title: Untitled
content:
Then he tackled him, sliding in from behind and locking him in a reverse choke hold. Twisting with all his might, Kratos wrenched the stranger’s neck until his head turned almost completely backward.
“Come on, do it! Of everyone I have faced, I’d hoped you would have been able to make me feel something, but even you can’t,” the stranger taunted, straining.
Kratos grunted and heaved, finally snapping the stranger’s neck. A moment later, his limp body crumpled to the ground before the God of War. Exhausted, Kratos dumped the stranger’s carcass into the crevasse. He stood there for many minutes waiting, half-expecting the man to come back to life and spring up from the hole to attack once more. As his breathing slowed to a normal rhythm, Kratos accepted that he had ended the strange man’s life.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
Then the stranger leapt across the gap, striking Kratos with a powerful uppercut, which launched him skyward. The stranger followed him up, and while Kratos scrabbled about on the ground, trying to regain his feet beneath him, the stranger stomped all over him before kicking him deep into the crevasse.
Kratos hit the bottom with a loud thud. He shook his head to clear his blurred vision. As he climbed the side of the crevasse, he could hear the stranger shouting.
“This fight is pointless. Your struggle is pointless. You cannot beat me.”
With trembling arms, Kratos emerged to assume a fighting stance across from the stranger.
“This again. Come on then.” The stranger’s smile crept all the way into Kratos’ soul.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
Kratos launched his full weight into the stranger, and they collided in the middle of the field. Both refused to budge. However, the thunderous fight caused the ground beneath them to give way; as the earth separated, they tumbled, still grappling, into the narrow space.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
“We are not done yet.” The stranger’s smile left his face.
The stranger elevated the monolith above his head and threw it at the God of War. Kratos caught the stone midair, and with a huge heave, launched it back. The stranger, in turn, caught it midflight, immediately charging at Kratos.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
Kratos leapt up to pull at the monolith with everything he could muster. It gave way, toppling on top of the stranger, crushing him beneath it.
Kratos heaved his chest to breathe, standing motionless for a moment.
It was over. Whoever this one was, he was dead now. Kratos could have spared his life, if he would have just walked away and left the God of War alone. He had no understanding of the type of person he had tangled with.
His body battered and aching, Kratos finally withdrew from the monolith, filling his lungs with deep, revitalizing breaths.
Five paces later he stopped. A low rumbling sounded. It could not be. That was impossible. No mortal could survive that.
“Leaving so soon?” The huge carving began to thunder as the stranger hoisted it above his head. He wore a relaxed smile.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
Rage consumed Kratos. Releasing a roar that reverberated through the forest, Kratos stormed across, grabbing the stranger by his throat and slamming him into the nearest tree. He repeatedly bashed him into the trunk until the tree teetered at an obtuse angle.
Tossing the stranger aside as if he were a bundle of rags, Kratos ripped the tree from the ground, ramming the rooted end into the man. Without so much as breaking a single stride, they crashed through the surrounding boulders and earth, ending up in a new area in the shadow of a massive carved stone monolith.
“Who are you afraid I will find?” the stranger shouted.
Kratos rammed the stranger into the monolith. He responded by jamming his fingers into the cracks of the tree trunk to tear it in half. Kratos began to realize his strength might be insufficient to defeat this one. The power this man controlled seemed far greater than any Kratos had encountered in his past life.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
Witnessing such supernatural power, the God of War ran at the stranger and grabbed him by the throat, dangling him off the ground.
“You are slow and old. You should never have come to Midgard,” the stranger taunted.
“You talk too much,” the God of War said.
Kratos pommeled the stranger’s face, intent on finishing him off before he might regain his strength. As the God of War’s strength began to wane, slowing his assault, the stranger leveraged Kratos’ shoulder to flip him rearward and reverse their positions. He unleashed a quick, powerful flurry of punches.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
Kratos commanded the frost and responded by launching his axe as hard as he could at the stranger, embedding it in the man’s chest, which forced him to take a knee. But he did not freeze! Somehow this one was unaffected by his axe’s most potent power. The act of Kratos recalling his axe caused the stranger to topple to the ground. In the next moment, the bleeding ceased, the wound closed up. Witnessing such supernatural power, the God of War ran at the stranger and grabbed him by the throat, dangling him off the ground.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
He slammed down on Kratos with both fists, sending him to the ground in a heap, then leapt to a nearby ridge where he lifted a boulder larger than himself.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
Kratos heaved up a nearby tree trunk, one damaged from their earlier exchange, and the stranger charged in response. When he was within range, Kratos swung the trunk in a wide arc, batting him back across the yard, where he crashed into the rake of the roof.
He had to prevent the stranger from entering his house and possibly uncovering the trapdoor. Above all else, he had to protect Atreus. Was that what this was all about? Did he intend to take or harm his son?
Kratos had to shut down his brain—act purely on instinct. As he raced toward the house, the stranger slid awkwardly off the roof. Kratos rammed him full force when the stranger hit the ground.
“Who are you hiding?” the stranger questioned, while repeatedly battering Kratos’ ribs.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
Kratos whacked the stranger’s unprotected jaw, while at the same time bucking the man off and onto the roof. The God of War dove on top of the flailing stranger, threw the man’s arms aside, and unleashed his own flurry of debilitating punches.
The stranger, however, recovered quickly, deflecting Kratos’ blows and using his superior strength to seize Kratos by the neck and fling him into the garden behind the house.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
When Kratos charged, the stranger leveraged the God of War’s superior weight against him, flinging him into the side of his house. He laughed when Kratos bounced off the structure to hurl himself at him.
The stranger responded by grabbing Kratos as if he were a ragdoll, leaping high into the air with him in his clutches to slam him into the overhanging roof. Straddled over Kratos, the stranger unleashed a flurry of rapid, debilitating punches, the unseeing gaze of a madman on his face.
“This is real simple. Tell me what I want, and the pain stops,” the stranger shouted.
Kratos worked his right arm free. Ramming his fist into the old man’s face again and again, then throwing him off, he slammed the stranger onto the roof so hard that the thatch gave way, revealing the room’s contents below through a gaping hole.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
The man’s visage turned up in a strange display of pleasure at what had occurred. “No. No, no, no, no. Fine. Now my turn,” the stranger said.
The stranger charged, launching a fierce uppercut, which sent Kratos flying into the air, skipping across the roof of his house, and finally coming to rest in his yard.
This was no ordinary man.
The stranger followed with an enormous leap, landing less than a dozen paces from the God of War.
Kratos rolled away, springing to his feet and planting them firmly to brace for another onslaught.
“How incredibly disappointing. Come on then,” the stranger taunted.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
Drawing back his fist, Kratos delivered a hard hook to the stranger’s jaw, which sent the man awkwardly to the ground on one knee.
“Why do you not heed my warning?” Kratos said.
As the stranger remained motionless on his knee, Kratos risked a glance over his shoulder at the house, wondering at that moment what, if anything, he should tell his son. This was not the person he wanted his son to know about. The Kratos-of-the-past was not the Kratos-of-the-present.
The stranger’s rising brought Kratos’ face back to him.
The man’s visage turned up in a strange display of pleasure at what had occurred. “No. No, no, no, no. Fine. Now my turn,” the stranger said.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
In a dizzying blur, the stranger attacked with a series of hard, wild punches, hurling his feeble weight into the God of War.
Kratos caught the fourth punch, crushing the man’s fist within his. “I warned you,” he snarled.
Unflinching, the stranger released a sigh of relief, waiting for what was to come. An elated smile formed across the man’s grimy face.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
Before Kratos could react, the stranger relaxed his fists. In the next second, he backslapped Kratos hard across his face.
Kratos held his stance, reminded himself of the vow he had made when he came to this land. He forced his arms to remain at his sides, though all his muscles were ready.
Fire bloomed in Kratos’ eyes. Feeling the uncontrollable rage returning, the rage he swore he would never revert to, he exhaled deeply, forcing himself to relax. He refused to allow this spineless woodsman to goad him into a fight.
“Leave my home now,” Kratos spat. The stranger only smiled, revealing carious teeth and his willingness to fight. Or was it a willingness to die?

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 4

title: Untitled
content:
Moments later a formidable gyrfalcon, half the size of Atreus with speckled black plumage and a seven-foot wingspan, soared out from the forest canopy, scattering the birds in different directions.
“Jöphie is back. I thought she had abandoned us after…”
Atreus outstretched his arm to attract the bird, which would easily consume his entire arm, to support her as a perch, but the bird of prey ignored his offering and settled onto a nearby tree stump.
“She only went to Mother. She never would come to me,” Atreus said, abandoning his attempt.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 3

title: Untitled
content:
Kratos leapt to his feet, throwing his axe up quickly enough to drive the blade into the troll’s shoulder.
An agonized wail shattered the forest stillness as the troll shifted his hand over the spurting wound.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 2

title: Untitled
content:
Kratos slammed his free fist into the troll’s jaw, knocking it back a few feet. The troll had seriously underestimated this man’s strength.
“Do not fire!” Kratos commanded.
Kratos charged, only to take a slamming fist to his chest, which drove him to the ground while the troll leaned over him with a sickening grin.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 2

title: Untitled
content:
Before Kratos could mount another attack, the troll slammed its full weight into him, casting him aside like a limp ragdoll and toppling the axe to the ground a short distance beyond reach. The beast then released a garbled laugh at the human’s feeble efforts.
But what the troll’s simple mind failed to comprehend how the axe returned a moment later, allowing Kratos to position the blade defensively.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 2

title: Untitled
content:
Raising an open palm, Kratos commanded his axe’s return. The weapon, magically bound to him, responded without hesitation, whipping back into his hand.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 2

title: Untitled
content:
“Kjöt,” the troll growled.
“What did he say?” Kratos asked out the side of his mouth.
“I think he said meat.” Atreus scrambled through crackling auburn leaves to retrieve his fallen bow.
“Dauði Kaupmaðr ta,” the troll snarled.
It lofted the carcass in victory, and with the deer’s limp head flopping, brought the neck toward its gaping jaws.
“No! You’re not taking our kill!” Atreus fired back, understanding the last word as “take”.
[...]
“Þú tilheyra ekki hér!” the troll uttered. Disgust lined its voice; contempt poured out of dark, soulless orbs.
“We do belong here!” Atreus shouted, amazed he’d deciphered the troll’s claim. His mother had anticipated that someday this exchange would come, and he should be prepared to handle it. “We hunt where we please.”

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 2

title: Untitled
content:
“No!” Kratos yelled, withdrawing the Leviathan axe strapped across his back. Issuing an unspoken command, he charged the axe with frost, aimed, then hurled it at the troll. The deer trunk came down to shield it from the strike. When the axe penetrated the stag’s hide it instantly froze it, causing the startled troll to release it. The carcass shattered into a hundred pieces when it hit the ground.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 2

title: Untitled
content:
Kratos started to back Atreus to safety when the troll lunged for them, slamming a massive fist into the God of War’s chest, while simultaneously lifting the limp deer with its other hand.
The attack sent Kratos and the boy tumbling into a hollow in the trees.

“Kjöt,” the troll growled.
“What did he say?” Kratos asked out the side of his mouth.
“I think he said meat.” Atreus scrambled through crackling auburn leaves to retrieve his fallen bow.

God Of War - Midgard, Ch 1-2

title: GTK3 dark theme settings for GnuCash on Windows 10
content:
# GTK3 dark theme settings for GnuCash on Windows 10
# File location: %LOCALAPPDATA%\gtk-3.0\settings.ini
# (C:\Users\%USERNAME%\AppData\Local\gtk-3.0\settings.ini)
#
# Enables dark mode in GTK3 apps like GnuCash using Adwaita (GNOME default theme with dark variant)
#
# If register text is unreadable (dark-on-dark) in GnuCash:
#   Edit → Preferences → Register
#   Untick "Use GnuCash built-in color scheme"
#   This lets the GTK theme control register colors
#
# Added/updated: February 16, 2026

[Settings]
gtk-application-prefer-dark-theme=true
gtk-theme-name=Adwaita

# Restart GnuCash after saving changes
